---
title: "Portal Customization"
pageId: "69ef0c339b4ca5aaa64976bc"
---

From the **Settings menu,** select **Portal Customization**.

The Portal Customization section includes two main tabs:

- **Hero Section** – For customizing the homepage header content and visuals.
- **Embedded Resources** – For adding resource cards that highlight important materials or links.

## **Hero Section**

The **Hero Section** enables you to customize the main banner displayed at the top of your documentation homepage.
This section typically includes a title, subtitle, description, and optional image \(see Figure 1\).

![](data:image/png;base64,)

#### **Key Elements**

- **Pre\-headline: **Displays a short introductory text above the main headline \(e.g., *DOCUWIZ*\). This usually represents the brand name or product name.
- **Site Headline: **The main heading of the portal’s landing page \(e.g., *DOCUWIZ API Documentation*\). This headline defines the purpose or title of your documentation.
- **Site Description: **A short paragraph summarizing the documentation’s purpose. Example: *“Your toolkit for building business apps, whether it’s a custom solution or a listing on the DOCUWIZ Marketplace.”*
- **Thumbnail: **Displays an optional image that visually represents your portal. This can be your brand image, logo, or an illustration.
- **Preview Section: **Displays a live preview of your documentation homepage, including text and image updates, so you can review changes before publishing.

### **Editing the Hero Section**

When you click the **Edit **\(**pencil** icon\) option, editable fields appear, allowing you to update the Hero Section \(see Figure 2\).

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Edit the following fields:

- **Pre\-headline: **Enter a short tagline \(maximum 50 characters\).
- **Site Headline: **Specify the main headline for your documentation \(maximum 80 characters\).
- **Site Description: **Write a concise description of your documentation portal \(up to 140 characters\).
- **Thumbnail Upload Area: **Upload a visual for your homepage by dragging and dropping a file or selecting **Browse Files**.
  - Supported formats: .png and .jpeg
  - Maximum file size: **2 MB**
- **Saving the Changes:**
  - **Save Changes:** Saves and applies the updated text and visuals.
  - **Cancel:** Discards edits without saving.
- After making changes, the updates automatically appear in the **Preview** section and on the **end\-user homepage**, reflecting your latest design and content adjustments in real time.

## **Embedded Resources**

The **Embedded Resources** tab lets you display helpful links or featured resources directly on your homepage. You can add up to **six \(6\)** resource cards, each containing an image, header, subheader, and link \(see Figure 3\).

![](data:image/png;base64,)

### **Adding a New Embedded Resource**

**Default View:**

- Displays an **Add Another Resource** card.
- Clicking the **\( \+ \) Add another resource** button opens the **Add Embedded Resource** window \(see figure 4\).

![](data:image/png;base64,)

### **Add Embedded Resource Window**

Allows you to create a new resource card with the following fields:

- **Image Upload: **Upload a visual representing the resource.
  - Supported formats: .png, .jpeg
  - Maximum file size: **2 MB**
  - Provides a visual cue for users visiting your portal.
- **Resource Header: **The title or name of the resource \(maximum 45 characters\). Example: *“Getting Started Guide”*
- **Subheader: **A short description or summary of the resource \(maximum 150 characters\). Example: *“Learn how to integrate our APIs quickly with this step\-by\-step guide.”*
- **Link: **Add a hyperlink that directs users to the actual resource or external documentation. Example: [https://docs.docuwiz.io/getting\-started](https://docs.docuwiz.io/getting-started)
- **Saving the New Resources:**
  - **Save:** Adds the resource card to your homepage.
  - **Cancel:** Exits the window without saving.
- Once saved, the new card is added to the **Embedded Resources** section, allowing you to highlight important links for users. 
- The embedded resource card is also displayed automatically in the **Additional Resources** section on the **end\-user homepage**.

>
